story.title: Dr. Chen and the Melting Ice Cream System
story.text:
Hello! I’m Dr. Sarah Chen, a research scientist. I study how different parts of a system work together. Let’s look at a delicious system: melting ice cream.

Why does it melt faster on a hot day? The answer is thermal energy. Warm air has a lot of this energy. This energy always moves from warm places to cool places. We call this movement heat transfer. When the air is very hot, the heat transfer to your cold ice cream is very fast.

This fast energy movement causes rapid melting. The solid ice cream quickly turns into a liquid. But what if you use a special cup? Good insulation in a container can help. Insulation is a barrier that slows down heat transfer. This keeps your ice cream frozen longer.

At my lab, I create models to see how these parts connect. Understanding this helps us design better coolers and even insulated homes.
think_abouts: What happens to the heat transfer rate if you put the ice cream in the sun?
How does a foam cup (insulation) change the melting speed?
story.go_deeper: How is the system of melting ice cream similar to the system of a snowman melting?

margin_defs.definition: simple ways to show something complex

check_prompts: What does insulation do to heat transfer?

vocabulary.definition: The movement of thermal energy from a warmer object to a cooler object.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Heat transfer from the hot air made the ice cream melt.

vocabulary.definition: A material that slows down heat transfer.
vocabulary.use_sentence: My lunch box has good insulation to keep my food cold.

vocabulary.definition: The change from solid to liquid caused by absorbing heat energy.
vocabulary.use_sentence: We watched the melting of the ice cube on the warm sidewalk.

vocabulary.definition: The total energy of moving particles in a substance; we feel it as heat.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The sun gives off a lot of thermal energy.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Chen designs better coolers. What other things could she help design by understanding heat transfer?
discussion_cards.frame: By understanding heat transfer, Dr. Chen could also help design a better ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Describe the 'ice cream system' using the words Air Temperature, Insulation, and Melting Speed.
discussion_cards.frame: When the Air Temperature is high, the Melting Speed is ___ unless there is good ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
